page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. Operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance allocates 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8azn.metal-24xl demonstrates top-tier performance across compute, memory, and network-heavy workloads. It achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory evaluations. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers high bandwidth, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 22,772.04 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 400.32 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, the system records top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations, alongside a Redis SET throughput of 334.99 million operations per second.

Network performance is a key feature of this instance, offering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. While the server lacks local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, its high-frequency dedicated cores and massive memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, non-GPU workloads. It is optimized for high-throughput network applications, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference, such as medium-sized 7B parameter LLM text generation where it maintains top-tier execution speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, the instance relies on its 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and EBS optimization. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It delivers exceptional throughput, including 334.99 million Redis SET operations per second and 152.9 GB/sec static web serving. This hardware profile is highly suited for network-optimized workloads, high-throughput database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 dedicated 5.0 GHz AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier compute and memory performance.

tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory with 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
